verdict_reasoning: EREG is a membrane-anchored EGF-family ligand that exists in two forms: a transmembrane precursor and a soluble shed ectodomain. The TM precursor undergoes regulated proteolytic cleavage by sheddases (ADAM10, ADAM17), releasing the soluble mature ligand. The precursor stage is documented on the cell surface but transient; the dominant biological actor is the shed soluble form. This fits the pattern of a transient TM proligand where juxtacrine signaling occurs from the membrane-anchored stage but constitutive and regulated shedding rapidly release the active ligand into the soluble pool. Surface accessibility is thus limited to the transient membrane-anchored precursor stage.
